title: Learning to sign 'good morning' for a colleague
summary: Reflect on how learning a few signs for a Deaf coworker changed a quiet, distant working relationship.
topic: Crossing a language barrier with a coworker by learning a few signs
prompt: Write 9–11 sentences reflecting on a time you learned a little of someone's language (sign language, a few words) to reach across a barrier with a coworker or neighbour. Explain what you learned and how it changed things. Use past simple, present perfect, and connectors.
hintStarters: For two years I worked beside Daniel without ever really…
He is Deaf, and at first we only communicated by…
One weekend I decided to learn a few signs because…
The first time I signed 'good morning,' his face…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bị bỏ rơi, bị gạt ra ngoài

sampleText: For two years I worked beside Daniel without ever really talking to him. He is Deaf, and at first we only communicated by short notes and simple gestures. We were polite, but there was always a quiet barrier between us. I noticed that at lunch he often sat a little apart, and I thought he might feel left out. One weekend I decided to learn a few signs, because I wanted to greet him in his own language. I watched some videos and practised in front of a mirror, and I felt clumsy and slow. On Monday morning I signed 'good morning' to him for the first time. His face lit up, and he signed back more quickly than I could follow. I made many mistakes, and he laughed kindly and corrected my hands. Since that day, I have learned about twenty signs, and we now have small conversations every morning. I am still very far from fluent, but the barrier feels much smaller than before. The lesson was simple: learning even a little of someone's language tells them that you see them. I had assumed it was Daniel's job to fit into our world, but the effort should go both ways. A small effort from me changed a relationship that two years of silence never could.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I worked beside Daniel without ever really talking to him
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi làm việc cạnh Daniel mà chưa bao giờ thật sự nói chuyện với anh ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: we only communicated by short notes and simple gestures
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chúng tôi chỉ giao tiếp bằng những mẩu giấy ngắn và cử chỉ đơn giản

sampleAnnotations.phrase: there was always a quiet barrier between us
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: luôn có một rào cản lặng lẽ giữa chúng tôi
anh ấy có thể cảm thấy bị bỏ rơi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I decided to learn a few signs
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi quyết định học vài ký hiệu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: practised in front of a mirror

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi cảm thấy vụng về và chậm chạp

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I signed 'good morning' to him for the first time
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi ký hiệu 'chào buổi sáng' với anh ấy lần đầu tiên
khuôn mặt anh ấy bừng sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: he laughed kindly and corrected my hands
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: anh ấy cười hiền và chỉnh lại tay tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I have learned about twenty signs
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi đã học khoảng hai mươi ký hiệu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the barrier feels much smaller than before
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: rào cản cảm thấy nhỏ hơn nhiều so với trước

sampleAnnotations.phrase: learning even a little of someone's language tells them that you see them
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: học dù chỉ một chút ngôn ngữ của ai đó cho họ biết rằng bạn nhìn thấy họ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the effort should go both ways
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nỗ lực nên đến từ cả hai phía

sampleAnnotations.phrase: two years of silence never could
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hai năm im lặng chưa bao giờ làm được

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect for a result that reaches now
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'Since that day, I have learned about twenty signs' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành với 'since' để cho thấy việc học bắt đầu trong quá khứ và kết quả vẫn đúng đến hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Since that day, I have learned about twenty signs' uses present perfect with 'since' to show the learning began in the past and the result still holds now.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an old assumption
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'I had assumed it was Daniel's job to fit into our world' dùng quá khứ hoàn thành để diễn tả một suy nghĩ tôi đã có trước khi câu chuyện thay đổi nó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had assumed it was Daniel's job to fit into our world' uses past perfect to express a belief I held before the story changed it.

sampleTranslationVi: Suốt hai năm tôi làm việc cạnh Daniel mà chưa bao giờ thật sự nói chuyện với anh ấy. Anh ấy khiếm thính, và lúc đầu chúng tôi chỉ giao tiếp bằng những mẩu giấy ngắn và cử chỉ đơn giản. Chúng tôi lịch sự, nhưng luôn có một rào cản lặng lẽ giữa chúng tôi. Tôi để ý rằng vào bữa trưa anh ấy thường ngồi hơi tách ra một chút, và tôi nghĩ anh ấy có thể cảm thấy bị bỏ rơi. Một cuối tuần tôi quyết định học vài ký hiệu, vì tôi muốn chào anh ấy bằng chính ngôn ngữ của anh. Tôi xem vài video và luyện tập trước gương, và tôi cảm thấy vụng về và chậm chạp. Sáng thứ Hai tôi ký hiệu 'chào buổi sáng' với anh ấy lần đầu tiên. Khuôn mặt anh ấy bừng sáng, và anh ký hiệu đáp lại nhanh hơn tôi có thể theo kịp. Tôi mắc nhiều lỗi, và anh cười hiền và chỉnh lại tay tôi. Từ ngày đó, tôi đã học khoảng hai mươi ký hiệu, và giờ chúng tôi có những cuộc trò chuyện nhỏ mỗi sáng. Tôi vẫn còn rất xa mức lưu loát, nhưng rào cản cảm thấy nhỏ hơn nhiều so với trước. Bài học rất đơn giản: học dù chỉ một chút ngôn ngữ của ai đó cho họ biết rằng bạn nhìn thấy họ. Tôi đã từng cho rằng việc hòa nhập vào thế giới của chúng tôi là nhiệm vụ của Daniel, nhưng nỗ lực nên đến từ cả hai phía. Một nỗ lực nhỏ từ tôi đã thay đổi một mối quan hệ mà hai năm im lặng chưa bao giờ làm được.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence about the distance at work.
options: For two years I worked beside Daniel without ever really talk to him.
For two years I work beside Daniel without ever really talking to him.
Since two years I worked beside Daniel without ever really talking to him.
For two years I worked beside Daniel without ever really talking to him.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without + verb-ing' (talking) and past 'worked' are correct. 'Talk' is wrong after 'without'; 'work' lacks past tense; 'since two years' should be 'for two years'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence describing how they first communicated.
options: We only communicated by short notes and simple gestures.
We only communicated with short notes and simple gestures by.
We only communicate by short notes and simple gestures.
We only communicated by short notes and simple gesture.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Communicated by + means' is correct, with plural 'gestures'. The second has a dangling 'by'; 'communicate' lacks past tense; 'gesture' should be plural.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition shows the means: 'by' or 'with'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence about his possible feeling is best written?
options: I thought he might feels left out.
I thought he might feel left out.
I thought he might to feel left out.
I thought he might felt left out.
mcQuestions.explanation: After the modal 'might' use the base verb 'feel'. 'Feels', 'to feel', and 'felt' are all wrong after 'might'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that gives the reason with 'because'.
options: I decided to learn a few signs because of I wanted to greet him.
I decided learning a few signs because I wanted to greet him.
I decided to learn a few signs because I wanted to greet him.
I decided to learn a few signs, because wanted to greet him.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Decided to learn ... because I wanted' is correct. 'Because of' needs a noun; 'decided learning' is wrong; the last drops the subject.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about how he reacted.
options: His face lighted up, and he signed back quickly.
His face lit up, and he signed back quick.
His face lit up, and he sign back quickly.
His face lit up, and he signed back quickly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Lit up' is the past of 'light up' and 'quickly' is the adverb. 'Lighted up' is unnatural here; 'quick' is an adjective; 'sign' lacks past tense.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the natural past of 'light up', and is 'quick' or 'quickly' correct after a verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence about what she has learned since then is best written?
options: Since that day, I have learned about twenty signs.
Since that day, I learned about twenty signs.
Since that day, I have learn about twenty signs.
For that day, I have learned about twenty signs.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect with 'since' shows a result reaching now. Past simple loses the link to now; 'have learn' is wrong; 'for that day' is the wrong preposition.
mcQuestions.hint: Which pairs with present perfect: 'since' or 'for'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct comparative about the barrier.
options: The barrier feels much more small than before.
The barrier feels much smaller than before.
The barrier feels much smaller as before.
The barrier feels much smallest than before.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smaller than' is the correct comparative. 'More small' is wrong; 'as' does not follow a comparative; 'smallest' is a superlative.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the comparative of 'small', and which word follows it?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that states the lesson naturally.
options: Learning a little of someone's language tell them that you see them.
Learn a little of someone's language tells them that you see them.
Learning a little of someone's language tells them that you see them.
Learning a little of someone's language telling them that you see them.
mcQuestions.explanation: The gerund subject 'Learning' takes the singular 'tells'. 'Tell' disagrees; 'Learn' is not a noun subject; 'telling' lacks a finite verb.
mcQuestions.hint: A gerund subject like 'Learning' is singular — which verb form follows?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence about her old assumption is best written?
options: I had assume it was Daniel's job to fit into our world.
I have assumed it was Daniel's job to fit into our world.
I had assumed it was Daniel's job for fit into our world.
I had assumed it was Daniel's job to fit into our world.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had assumed' fits a belief held before the change, with 'to fit'. 'Had assume' is wrong; present perfect shifts to now; 'for fit' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: The assumption came before the story changed it — which past form fits?

options: A small effort from me changed a relationship that silence never could.
A small effort from me changed a relationship that silence never could change it.
A small effort from me change a relationship that silence never could.
A small effort from me changed a relationship what silence never could.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A relationship that silence never could' correctly leaves the verb understood after 'could'. The second repeats the object; 'change' lacks past tense; 'what' should be 'that'.
mcQuestions.hint: Should the object be repeated after 'could', and is it 'that' or 'what'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: When two people do not share a language, whose job is it to cross the gap? What does making the first small effort say about how you see the other person?
